summary: Closed, apparently unmerged PR that replaced several itlugia.c placeholder declarations with matched C implementations for Lugia item behavior. The main advertised match was itLugia_UnkMotion2_Anim, with additional implementations for it_802D16D4 and it_2725_Logic40_Spawned, plus a header prototype refinement for it_802D16D4.
what_changed: Implemented itLugia_UnkMotion2_Anim(Item_GObj* gobj), replacing a placeholder comment.

itLugia_UnkMotion2_Anim gets the Item with GET_ITEM(gobj), obtains the root HSD_JObj from gobj->hsd_obj, safely selects jobj->child only when jobj is non-null, calls it_8027A160(bone, item), and returns false.
Implemented it_802D16D4(Item_GObj* gobj), replacing an unknown placeholder.
it_802D16D4 calls Item_80268E5C((HSD_GObj*) gobj, 3, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE) and assigns the item hitlag callbacks entered_hitlag = efLib_PauseAll and exited_hitlag = efLib_ResumeAll.
Implemented it_2725_Logic40_Spawned(Item_GObj* gobj), replacing a placeholder.
it_2725_Logic40_Spawned calls it_8027ADEC(0x466, gobj, HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(gobj), it_804DD490), then it_802D23F4(gobj).
Updated itlugia.h prototype for it_802D16D4 from UNK_RET it_802D16D4(UNK_PARAMS) to void it_802D16D4(Item_GObj* gobj).
smart_moves: The itLugia_UnkMotion2_Anim implementation preserves an explicit null check before accessing jobj->child, which is a common matching-sensitive pattern and avoids folding the access into unsafe shorthand.
The header prototype was tightened once the function body established the real signature, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with void and Item_GObj*.
The hitlag setup in it_802D16D4 reuses existing item/effect callbacks efLib_PauseAll and efLib_ResumeAll rather than introducing local wrapper logic.
The spawn handler keeps the observed call ordering: effect/object setup through it_8027ADEC followed by post-spawn initialization via it_802D23F4.
decomp_lessons: When matching item animation callbacks, preserve bool return conventions even when the function only performs side effects; this implementation returns false after calling it_8027A160.
For item functions that operate on render bones, the root HSD object may be accessed from gobj->hsd_obj and then its child selected conditionally; matching may require spelling out the NULL branch.
Once a placeholder function is matched, immediately propagate its concrete signature to the corresponding item header to remove UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS.
Item hitlag behavior can be represented by assigning Item fields entered_hitlag and exited_hitlag to efLib pause/resume callbacks after setting the animation state with Item_80268E5C.
Spawned logic callbacks may combine HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(gobj) with item-specific constants and global data such as it_804DD490; preserve literal constants when no symbolic name is evidenced.
naming_conventions: Existing item callback names use the pattern itLugia_UnkMotionN_Anim, itLugia_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itLugia_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Address-derived internal functions retain names like it_802D16D4, it_2725_Logic40_Spawned, and it_802D23F4 when no semantic name is provided.
Header comments retain address annotations such as /* 2D16D4 */ next to prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit local variables for HSD_JObj* bone, Item* item, and HSD_JObj* jobj in itLugia_UnkMotion2_Anim; this likely helped preserve register/lifetime structure for matching.
Represent gobj->hsd_obj as an explicit cast to HSD_JObj* before reading child.
Use a branchy NULL check assigning bone = NULL or bone = jobj->child instead of a compact ternary or direct access, likely to match generated control flow.
Cast Item_GObj* to HSD_GObj* when passing to Item_80268E5C, matching the callee signature while keeping the item-specific callback signature.
Leave literal 0x466 in it_2725_Logic40_Spawned because the diff provides no symbolic constant.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so the slice does not prove the submitted code was accepted as-is.
follow_up_queries: Check whether a later PR reimplemented or merged these same itlugia.c functions after PR #2096 was closed unmerged.
Search for it_802D16D4 in current master to verify whether the void Item_GObj* signature survived.
Compare itLugia_UnkMotion2_Anim against neighboring item animation callbacks that call it_8027A160 for reusable matching patterns.
Look for symbolic meaning of literal 0x466 used in it_2725_Logic40_Spawned.
